sections.paragraph:
ἑτεροδοξία, ας, ἡ (s. prec. entry; Pla., Theaet. 193d; Philo, Fgm. 72 Harris) expression of thought that differs from accepted views or teachings, with implication of being divisive, strange, erroneous opinion IMg 8:1.—TW.

prec. prec. = preceding

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

IMg IMg = Ignatius to the Magnesians—List 1

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 399
